rationale: Materials Science is the youngest of the level-1 categories and the most explicitly interdisciplinary. Twelve subspecialties split by material class (metals, ceramics, polymers, 2D, biomaterials) and by application function (batteries, photovoltaics). I added Materials by Design (computational + ML) as its own card because the materials-discovery-by-ML community has crystallized rapidly. I deliberately did NOT split Nanomaterials as a separate card — nano cuts across all material classes and would make every other card a 'subset of nano'.

subspecialties.name: Metals & Alloys
subspecialties.tagline: Steels, high-entropy alloys, processing, mechanical behavior.
covers: steel, aluminum, titanium, high-entropy alloys, mechanical metallurgy, additive manufacturing of metals, phase transformations

subspecialties.name: Ceramics & Glasses
covers: functional ceramics, structural ceramics, glasses, refractory materials, oxide thin films, glass-ceramics

subspecialties.name: Polymeric & Soft Materials
subspecialties.tagline: Engineering plastics, elastomers, gels, soft robotics.
covers: engineering plastics, elastomers, hydrogels, organogels, soft actuators, polymer composites
Macromolecules applied, Polymer, ACS Macro Lett

subspecialties.name: Composites
covers: fiber-reinforced composites, CFRPs, hybrid composites, multifunctional composites, composite manufacturing

subspecialties.name: Nanomaterials & Thin Films
subspecialties.tagline: Nanoparticles, nanowires, thin film growth, characterization.
covers: nanoparticle synthesis/application, nanowires/nanotubes, thin film deposition (ALD, CVD, MBE), nanofabrication, in situ characterization

subspecialties.name: 2D & Quantum Materials
covers: graphene, transition metal dichalcogenides, moiré superlattices, topological insulators (materials angle), 2D heterostructures
explicitly_excludes: physics theory of quantum materials → Physics & Astronomy

subspecialties.name: Energy Materials
subspecialties.tagline: Battery, solar cell, fuel cell, hydrogen storage materials.
covers: battery cathodes/anodes/electrolytes, perovskite solar cells, photovoltaic materials, hydrogen storage, fuel cell materials, thermoelectrics
Adv Energy Mater, Nat Energy materials, Joule materials

subspecialties.name: Biomaterials & Tissue Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Scaffolds, implants, drug delivery materials, bio-interfaces.
covers: scaffolds, implant materials, bio-interfaces, drug delivery materials, regenerative materials, dental and orthopedic biomaterials

subspecialties.name: Magnetic & Electronic Materials
covers: magnetic thin films, spintronic materials, ferroelectrics, multiferroics, dielectrics, piezoelectrics, semiconductor materials

subspecialties.name: Structural Materials & Mechanics
covers: mechanical behavior of materials, fracture mechanics, fatigue, multi-scale mechanics modeling, structural composites mechanics
explicitly_excludes: solid mechanics theory → Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering

subspecialties.name: Materials by Design & Computational Materials
subspecialties.tagline: DFT, ML for materials, high-throughput screening, autonomous discovery.
covers: DFT for materials, ML for materials property prediction, high-throughput screening, autonomous experimentation, generative materials models
npj Comput Mater, Comput Mater Sci
explicitly_excludes: pure ML methodology → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Characterization & Microscopy
subspecialties.tagline: TEM, STEM, X-ray, atom probe, in-situ.
covers: electron microscopy methods, X-ray characterization (XRD, XPS), atom probe tomography, in-situ characterization, neutron scattering for materials, scanning probe

uncovered_concerns: Considered Smart/Stimuli-Responsive Materials as its own card — distributed across Polymeric, Magnetic, Biomaterials; not its own slot.
Considered Sustainability/Recyclable Materials — overlaps Green Chemistry; for now folded into the relevant material class.
Considered Construction/Civil Materials (concrete, asphalt) — small in research-paper volume; folds under Ceramics or Composites here.
